text: Queen’s University offers a wide range of graduate programs and degrees through the School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs (SGSPA), including Doctoral (PhD) programs, Master’s programs, and Graduate Diplomas. The Academic Calendar outlines important rules, regulations, and deadlines for all graduate students.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Grad Maps provide program-specific guidance to help students navigate academics, research, networking, and career development at Queen’s and beyond. Physical Grad Maps are available at the SGSPA office.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Health Sciences Graduate Studies programs include: Aging and Health (G.Dip., M.Sc., PhD); Biomedical & Molecular Sciences – Research Fields (M.Sc., PhD); Biomedical & Molecular Sciences – Anatomical Sciences (M.Sc.); Biostatistics (M.Sc. specializing in Biostatistics); Cancer Research (M.Sc., PhD specializing in Cancer Research); Combined Medicine with Graduate Research (M.D./PhD, M.D./Master’s); Epidemiology (M.Sc., PhD); Health Professions Education (MHPE); Health Quality Programs (M.Sc. (HQ), PhD (HQ)); Medical Sciences (G.Dip., M.M.Sc., currently inactive); Neuroscience (M.Sc., PhD); Nursing (G.Dip., PHCNP Dip, MNSc., MN(PHCNP), PhD); Occupational Therapy (M.Sc.OT); Pathology & Molecular Medicine (M.Sc., PhD); Pharmaceutical & Healthcare Management & Innovation (G.Dip.); Physical Therapy (M.Sc.PT); Public Health (M.PH); Rehabilitation and Health Leadership (D.Sc.); Rehabilitation Science (M.Sc., PhD); Translational Medicine (M.Sc., PhD).

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Arts and Science Graduate Studies programs include: Art Conservation – Research Stream (M.A.C.); Art Conservation – Treatment Stream (M.A.C.); Art History (M.A., PhD); Arts Leadership (M.A.); Arts Management (Grad Dip.); Astrophysics & Astronomy (M.Sc., PhD); Biology (M.Sc., PhD); Biomedical Informatics (Grad Dip., MBI); Chemistry (M.Sc., M.A.Sc., PhD); Classics (M.A.); Computing (M.Sc., PhD); Cultural Studies (M.A., PhD); Earth and Energy Resources Leadership (M.E.E.R.L.); Economics (M.A., PhD); Economics – combined with Law (M.A. & J.D.); English Language & Literature (M.A., M.Phil, PhD); Environmental Studies (M.E.S., PhD); Gender Studies (M.A., PhD); Geological Sciences (M.Sc., PhD); Geography (M.A., M.Sc., PhD); Global Development Studies (M.A., PhD); History (M.A., PhD); Industrial Relations (M.I.R., M.I.R./JD); Professional Industrial Relations (P.M.I.R.); Kinesiology & Health Studies (M.A., M.Sc., PhD); Mathematics and Statistics (M.Sc., PhD); Philosophy (M.A., PhD); Physics (M.Sc., PhD); Political Studies (M.A., PhD); Political and Legal Thought (M.A. specialization); Psychology (M.Sc., PhD); Public Administration (M.P.A., M.P.A./JD); Religious Studies (M.A.); Screen Cultures and Curatorial Studies (M.A., PhD); Sociology (M.A., PhD); Urban & Regional Planning (M.PL.).

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Smith Engineering and Applied Science Graduate and Professional Studies programs include: Applied Sustainability (M.A.Sc., M.Eng. specializing); Biomedical Engineering (M.A.Sc., PhD specializing); Chemical Engineering (M.Eng., M.A.Sc., PhD); Civil Engineering (M.Eng., M.A.Sc., PhD); Electrical & Computer Engineering (M.Eng., M.A.Sc., PhD); Engineering & Applied Physics (M.A.Sc., PhD); GeoEngineering (M.A.Sc., PhD); Geological Engineering (M.A.Sc., PhD); Mathematics & Engineering (M.A.Sc., PhD); Mechanical & Materials Engineering (M.Eng., M.A.Sc., PhD); Mining Engineering (M.Eng., M.A.Sc., PhD); Social Performance Management in the Extractive Industries (G.Dip.); UNENE – Nuclear Engineering (M.Eng.).

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Law Graduate Studies programs include: Law (LLM, LLM specializing in Political & Legal Thought, PhD); Graduate Diploma in Immigration and Citizenship Law (G.Dip.). The Faculty of Law also offers a Juris Doctor (JD) as a first professional law degree.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Smith School of Business Graduate Programs include Management (M.Sc., PhD). Additional graduate programs include Master of Business Administration, Master of Finance, Master of Financial Innovation & Technology, Master of International Business, Master of Management Analytics, Master of Management in Artificial Intelligence, Master of Management Innovation & Entrepreneurship, Graduate Diploma in Business, and Graduate Diploma in Accounting.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Faculty of Education Graduate Studies programs include Education (M.Ed., M.Ed. (WISE), PhD); Graduate Diploma in Education (GDE); Professional Master of Education (PME); and Ladder Your Credentials (Post-Graduate Certificate, Graduate Diploma, PME).

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Queen’s University School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs (SGSPA) welcomes and encourages applications from Indigenous candidates. The University supports Indigenous students through financial opportunities, admission regulations, and counselling services. Queen’s is committed to fostering inclusivity and reflection while acknowledging its participation in the colonial tradition, and continues taking steps to break down barriers and repair relationships with Indigenous peoples.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Queen’s waives the international student tuition differential for students who are verified members of Indigenous nations in the United States of America. This commitment reflects recognition of the spirit of the Jay Treaty of 1794.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: The Four Directions Indigenous Student Centre serves as a central hub for Indigenous students, offering cultural programming, community connections, and organizations to join. The Centre highlights the importance of voluntary and confidential self-identification.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: The Office of Indigenous Initiatives (OII), led by Associate Vice-Principal (Indigenous Initiatives and Reconciliation) Kanonhsyonne (Lisa Maracle), advances reconciliation and conciliation efforts at Queen’s. The office integrates Indigenous ways of knowing and being into university life, coordinates academic initiatives, and provides student assistance, funding opportunities, and programs that support decolonizing and indigenizing processes.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Queen’s University is committed to fostering an inclusive and reflective campus environment aligned with the recommendations of the Truth and Reconciliation Commission (TRC) Task Force. Campus initiatives continue to expand to ensure the TRC remains central to the University’s present and future, with many faculty members focusing on Indigenous histories, rights, perspectives, and cultures.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Queen’s supports Indigenous graduate students through several funding initiatives outlined on the Awards and Bursaries webpage. Self-identification is critical for accessing financial support. Individual bands distribute post-secondary funding differently and maintain their own deadlines; applicants are encouraged to contact the education representative at their band office for further information.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs
